ogTitle: Chermside West vs Cherry Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chermside West and Cherry Gully.
heading: Chermside West vs Cherry Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chermside West, Queensland and Cherry Gully, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chermside West has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Cherry Gully (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chermside West or Cherry Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chermside West has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Cherry Gully scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chermside West scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Cherry Gully scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chermside West or Cherry Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chermside West has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
